opp.ais_why:
A transcribed 35-min discovery call (2 June, after the prior data snapshot) shows active evaluation—two RFIs (sourcing + Zycus Datacore), demo planning, a business case—but no AI, Merlin or agentic capability was requested by the buyer.
Discussion centred on fragmented platforms, SAP ECC/S4HANA, IBM Cognos reporting and sourcing cycle-time pain; AI excitement was not expressed.
Account-level Avoma sweep confirms this is the only transcribed call; single-threaded, MEDDPICC empty. Medium confidence—evidence exists but shows no AI demand.

Evidence of advocacy: she is driving the RFI process, requested the demo topics form structure, asked for budgetary pricing and value engineering tooling to support KDP's internal business case, and engaged in detailed discovery (23 May 2026 call covering ERP landscape, integration needs, evaluation process). She has NOT yet demonstrated access to the Economic Buyer (CPO/CFO) or Decision Maker, and has not introduced Zycus to Michele (her boss, the bridge to C-level). Current engagement: active as of 23 May 2026 discovery call, but the scheduled demo 18 Jun 2026 was a no-show/bot-denied with no follow-up captured. competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the primary competitor, named by Marilia on the discovery call (23 May 2026: 'We are also looking at Ariba'). IBM Cognos was noted by the Zycus rep on the same call as a competitor for analytics/reporting use cases. No other competitors mentioned in calls or SF fields (Competitors__c not populated). Wedge against Ariba: Zycus Merlin (agentic AI for autonomous sourcing and intake) vs Ariba's non-agentic workflow automation; Zycus dual ERP integration (S/4HANA + ECC) as a tailored fit vs Ariba's SAP-native bias that may not serve the non-migrated ECC instances. Against Cognos: Zycus embedded savings tracking and supplier risk analytics vs standalone BI tool requiring integration. How we win: make Merlin Intake and autonomous sourcing the Phase 1 centerpiece in the rescheduled demo, and position Zycus as the only vendor purpose-built for procurement transformation across a mixed ERP estate post-acquisition. The competitive read is current as of 23 May 2026; no recent update (demo no-show 18 Jun).
